title: Police are searching for North Shore suspect in string of indecent assaults
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Revere Police are searching for a Hispanic male suspect linked to multiple indecent assaults in the Beachmont area.
The assaults occurred between January and March near Beachmont MBTA Station, involving inappropriate contact from behind.
Residents urged to review surveillance footage; tips can be reported to Revere Police at 781-286-1212 or 911.

title: Wu announces millions in new funding for immigrant services
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu announced a public-private partnership, securing over $4.4 million for immigrant services in Boston.
Funding will support legal aid, mental health services, resource delivery, language learning, and 'know your rights' courses for immigrants.
Three major foundations and Boston's city government are contributing to this initiative, criticized Trump-era immigration policies.

title: Report: Flag football game featuring Tom Brady moved out of Saudi Arabia
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Fanatics Flag Football Classic featuring Tom Brady was moved from Saudi Arabia to BMO Stadium, Los Angeles, due to Middle Eastern tensions.
The game, scheduled for March 21, will be captained by Tom Brady and Jalen Hurts, and broadcast on Fox Sports.
The move follows US-Israel military actions against Iran and an Iranian drone strike on the US Embassy in Riyadh.

title: Tell us: What’s been Joe Mazzulla’s best soundbite?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla known for memorable soundbites, asking fans to choose the best one.
Mazzulla's notable quotes range from personal opinions to comments on team pressures.
Selected soundbite will feature in Boston.com's annual sports bracket contest starting next week.

title: Why this Boston Marathon qualifier is running for the American Heart Association
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Blake Smith, 28, from Church Hill, Tennessee, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the American Heart Association.
Smith was diagnosed with Idiopathic Hypertrophic Subaortic Stenosis as a child but overcame it to become an avid runner.
He qualified for the Boston Marathon and is now raising funds for heart health, a cause personal to him.

title: Cohasset man charged after allegedly shooting family dog while hunting coyotes from his window
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Christopher Lucas, 59, was charged after allegedly shooting his family's dog while hunting coyotes from a window.
The incident occurred on Aug. 16, 2025, on Hillside Drive and was captured on home security cameras.
Lucas faced charges including animal cruelty, illegal firearm discharge, and hunting violations; firearms were seized from his home.

title: Greater Boston location of Uno Pizzeria reportedly saved by general manager
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Revere's Uno Pizzeria & Grill has reopened after closing in January, thanks to general manager Amalia Korom's intervention.
Korom bought and revamped the Revere location, rehiring all previous staff, maintaining its 34-year presence at Northgate Shopping Center.
The location is now the only Greater Boston Uno Pizzeria, among eight remaining New England locations after other closures.

title: Mass. business confidence turns positive for the first time in a year
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts business confidence turns positive, with February's AIM Index at 52, attributed to AI-driven productivity and profit gains.
The positivity in business outlook occurred ahead of recent Middle East tensions, which may impact energy prices and supply chains.
Employers remain worried about costs like energy, housing, and health care, despite recent state legislation to address rising expenses.

title: NFL analyst is not a fan of the Patriots’ reported free agent deal with Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots have agreed to a three-year, $48 million deal with former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker.
Analyst Daniel Popper criticized the signing due to Vera-Tucker's injury history, giving the deal a grade of D.
The Patriots traded center Garrett Bradbury, creating a role for Vera-Tucker, who is expected to play guard.

title: TD Garden owner sues cannabis dispensary The Boston Garden
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Delaware North, owner of TD Garden, is suing The Boston Garden dispensary for trademark infringement.
The dispensary, opened in 2021, was co-founded by actor Jonathan Tucker and Ivelise Rivera.
Delaware North claims the dispensary's name could confuse the public, associating it with Boston's sports arena.
